codeReview.summary: The feature cleanly adds a dedicated SensitiveValueOf<T> seam for enum-bearing payloads inside secure containers, threads it through the SensitiveShape runtime path, and keeps direct #[secure] enum syntax outside the supported contract. The focused runtime and Store regressions match the feature scope and show the seam composes with existing encrypted-at-rest behavior rather than bypassing it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker procedure says to add or update focused failing tests before implementation, but this worker transcript contains only passing baseline/focused validation commands and no pre-implementation failing proof. The feature still appears correct, but the skill procedure and actual workflow diverged.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 9905f5c6-b6de-4818-826a-69ac51d1197e shows cargo test baseline plus focused passing tests, with skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true and no recorded failing-first step.

summary: Pass. Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 85d7ccef687d43ca175ea87a1b3004b20b7f0901. The new SensitiveValueOf<T> runtime/storage seam is implemented in core/src/lib.rs, accepted by the Sensitive derive in macros/src/lib.rs, and covered by focused runtime/store tests without expanding scope to direct secure-enum syntax. No blocking or non-blocking code defects found.
